name: slice_infinite
description: Slice tool (mesh.sliceTool, task 0270 S4) `infinite` bool. The SAME drawn line is cut with infinite ON vs OFF on a mesh WIDER than the line, so the two visibly differ. Mesh: two disjoint co-planar quad strips in the y=0 plane — LEFT x in [-3,-1] (2 quads), RIGHT x in [1,3] (2 quads). The cut plane is z=0 (normal +Z, from a line along X perpendicular to the +Y work plane). The drawn line is Start(-4,0,0)->End(0,0,0): it spans ONLY the left strip (left crossings x=-3,-2,-1 fall within the segment's projected band; right crossings x=1,2,3 fall past End). infinite=ON extends the line indefinitely, so the plane slices the WHOLE mesh: all 4 quads split, +6 crossing verts (x=-3,-2,-1,1,2,3 at z=0), 12v/4f -> 18v/8f. infinite=OFF (the reference factory default) CLIPS the cut to the drawn span, so ONLY the left strip is cut: 2 quads split, +3 crossing verts (x=-3,-2,-1), 12v/4f -> 15v/6f; the right strip is untouched. Analytic self-golden (Mesh.cutByPlane / cutByPlaneClipped are connectivity-correct by construction, task 0108); the on/off boundary rule is a documented vibe3d divergence (the reference default is authoritatively OFF/clipped per the tool's own attribute description, but its exact per-face boundary behavior is uncapturable on the cube kit — see toolcards findings).
source: Analytic. Two 2-quad strips (y=0, z=+/-0.5). Cut plane z=0 (normal +Z). New crossing verts sit at (x,0,0) — the z-midpoint (t=0.5) of each x-const strip edge. Line Start(-4,0,0)->End(0,0,0): band s = dot(q-Start, End-Start)/|End-Start|^2 in [0,1] admits x in [-4,0] (left strip in, right strip out).

provenance.harness: hand-derived closed-form plane law (source/math.d planeFromLineAndWorkplane / planeForSlice)
